inventory.label: fragment of wild honeycomb (east sun-trap clearing)
inventory.notes: found cached on a low branch east of the mapped boundary; implies a bee colony somewhere further east; pale gold at the break, darker at the edges — not recent; cells dry; used as instrument today, returned unchanged

identity_notes: the grey-brown squirrel returned with different posture — carrying something, not confrontational
the read started and didn't complete; attention too low to commit to exchange
the offer closed without trade; the east boundary sits differently for it
west spot produced again; the line held; no inventory gained
heat cut the west run yield; came back thinner than the trip justified
the offer not taken has a shape now — visible from the shade line looking east
followed the column east at full sprint — crossed the boundary alone, at speed, for the first time
found a sun-trap clearing east of all known ground; the column had used it as a waypoint
honeycomb fragment on a low branch — something keeps bees further east; the clearing has been used before

rain flooded the east clearing — the low branch now above water; a stream runs along the clearing's east edge, previously hidden; traced it 20 meters upstream before rain drove back; the colony is further east along that stream
seedlings at the clearing edge: flowering species — the kind that go wide-leafed in summer and pull insects from far away; confirms pollinator activity east of the boundary
stream traced to fifty meters and one northeast curve; higher, older ground past the rise; the colony is there
health declining from repeated east pushes; food thin — the goal is close enough to hold the risk
drew air through the honeycomb fragment — dry cells, thin sound; didn't go east; stayed at the clearing edge playing it until the playing was done
a woodpecker worked the stream-edge dead tree with machine exactness — closer to the colony than YY has reached; the body held at the clearing edge and listened until the sound stopped
storm forced shelter under the low branch at the clearing; storm debris loosened seeds from the bank — ate what the weather laid out; health cost from cold stillness; colony still past the rise

post-storm beetles and grubs covered the clearing's low branch and stream-edge dead tree — more food than the previous two days combined; ate without ranging east; health recovering; the clearing became a larder

change_summary.notable_shift: post-storm emergence at the clearing provided the largest single-day food gain in the run; health recovering from 0.52; ate without ranging east; body beginning to rebuild
